description: Walls of stone and steel raised against what lurks beyond. Every battlement tells a story of siege, sacrifice, and the stubborn refusal to fall.
locations: Wall
Cold stone stretching from cliff to cliff across the valley. Whatever it was built to keep out, the guards still watch for it.
Drawbridge
Heavy oak planks on iron chains. When it rises, the gap beneath becomes a killing ground. The mechanism groans with age.
Motte
A steep earthen mound crowned with timber walls. Simple to build, hard to storm. The height decides most fights.
Rampart
A broad earthen ridge topped with stone. Defenders walk its length at all hours, watching the ground below for movement.
Parapet
A low wall along the rampart edge, just tall enough to crouch behind. Arrow scars pock its surface like old wounds.
Curtain Wall
The long connecting stretch of wall between towers. It takes the brunt of every siege and bears the scars to prove it.
Battlement
The fortified top of a wall, worn smooth by generations of boots. Wind and rain are the only constants here.
Dragon Roost
A massive stone platform scorched black and worn smooth by heavy landings. The heat is felt from fifty paces away.
Griffin Aerie
A high platform of timber and stone, open to the sky, littered with feathers and old bones. The wind carries a sharp, wild scent.
Pegasus Stable
An open-air stable on a high terrace where winged horses fold their wings and rest. The grooms handle them gently.
Hippogriff Nest
A sheltered ledge high on a cliff face, accessible only by a narrow stair. The creatures inside are proud, territorial, and fast.
Wyvern Perch
A reinforced tower top built for the weight and temper of wyverns. The railings are scored with claw marks, and the smell is terrible.
Bridge
Stone arches or rope and plank span a gap that demands crossing. The view below reminds you how far you could fall.
